title: Risk-First RBAC Design Doc
competency: 02. Spec Framing - Requirements decomposition and testable spec creation
domain: RBAC for customer data exports and privileged approvals
mission: Create a design doc for role-based access without overbuilding permissions or leaving privacy risks implicit.
outcome: RBAC is framed as risk control and implemented as enforceable behavior, not a diagram-only permission idea.
seededDefects: billing analyst can export PII without audit reason
team admin can grant owner role
denied actions do not emit audit events
agentWorkflow: Ask the coding agent to inspect this lab contract, starter code, docs, and tests before proposing a plan.
Revise the agent plan so it exercises the competency practice and avoids the common mistake.
Implement the smallest working change that addresses the seeded defects.
Run the verification gates and capture command evidence before writing the final review note.
workingDeliverables: Permission matrix encoded in code or config.
Audit event behavior for denied or privileged actions.
A concise design note explaining risk decisions.
masterySignals: Have the agent interview the RBAC request first: product questions, assumptions, non-goals, and missing examples.
Derive PR-sized slices, roles, resources, risky actions, and concrete acceptance criteria from the policy fixtures.
Implement route/action guards in the React starter and mock API enforcement for the selected slice.
Add tests for allowed, denied, audit-required, privacy-sensitive, and edge-case flows, then keep the spec session-readable.
